calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR A Status Report Is Set For The Filing Of The 3rd Accounting.

Petition For Authority To Extend Time To Close The Administration Of The Estate
Off Calendar Apr-22-2026 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Vincent Yu appeared via zoom for and with Lauro Trias (petitioner).

From the outset of the hearing, the Court indicated its preliminary intent to deny the petition and order the administrator to file a new accounting petition.

Counsel stated that a new accounting petition has not been filed since the Court denied the administrator's prior first interim account without prejudice because the value of the decedent's burial plot needed for the accounting has not been appraised.

The Court denied the petition and set a status hearing for June 17, 2026, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204, on the filing of a new petition with the administrator's first interim account covering a 2-year period from the date of death in January 2022. Counsel was directed to include the administrator's third report of status of administration in his new accounting petition.

Judge: Michael McNaughton. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Petition For Authority To Extend Time To Close The Administration Of The Estate
Off Calendar Apr-08-2026 Continued To Apr-22-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
Off Calendar Mar-17-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Vincent Yu for administrator Lauro Trias, who was also present (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on First Account and Report of Administrator on Decedent; Petition for Its Settlement and Approval; Petition for Approval of Status of Administration of Case; Petition for Authority to Extend Time to Close Administration of Estate.

The Court noted that some of the issues previously raised by the probate examiner remained outstanding despite several continuances. Mr. Yu stated that certain matters had been addressed and that others related to the estate real property.

The Court found the petition incomplete and denied the petition without prejudice. The Court directed that a new petition be filed addressing the outstanding issues.

The Court advised that the petition set for April 8, 2026, to extend time to close the estate may not be granted absent the filing of a new accounting petition or waivers of account from all beneficiaries.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Authority To Extend Time To Close The Administration Of The Estate
Off Calendar Feb-11-2026 Continued To Apr-08-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
Off Calendar Feb-11-2026 Continued To Mar-17-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Vincent Yu for administrator Lauro Trias, who was also present (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on First Account and Report of Administrator and Petition for Its Settlement, Petition for Approval of Status of Administration, and Petition for Authority to Extend Time to Close Administration of Estate.

Mr. Yu reported that a response to the probate examiner's notes had been filed late the prior afternoon addressing inventory issues relating to conservatorship assets and questions regarding fees and charges reflected in the accounting.

The Court noted that the filing had been verified by counsel rather than by the administrator and stated that verification by the administrator was required.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to March 17, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and directed that a properly verified response be filed.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
Off Calendar Jan-14-2026 Continued To Feb-11-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Vincent Yu for petitioner and administrator Lauro Trias, who was also present (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on First Account and Report of Administrator on Decedent; Petition for Its Settlement and Approval; Petition for Approval of Status of Administration of Case; Petition for Authority to Extend Time to Close Administration of Estate.

The Court reviewed the probate examiner notes and noted that the outstanding issues identified therein had been provided to counsel approximately seven months earlier and remained unaddressed.

Mr. Yu requested additional time to address the outstanding issues and file supplemental documentation.

The Court continued the matter to February 11, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The Court advised that failure to timely and adequately address the noted deficiencies may result in the petition being taken off calendar.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Authority To Extend Time To Close The Administration Of The Estate
Off Calendar Jan-14-2026 Continued To Feb-11-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
Off Calendar Oct-08-2025 Continued To Jan-14-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Vincent Yu for administrator Lauro Trias (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on First Account and Report of Administrator.

The Court noted that examiner notes remained unresolved and that counsel had since filed a separate petition for additional time, set for hearing on January 14, 2026. Mr. Yu explained that the delay stemmed from issues surrounding continued occupancy of the estate's real property. After multiple unsuccessful attempts to have the occupants vacate, a landlord-tenant attorney was retained in mid-September to proceed with eviction. Mr. Yu requested additional time to address the examiner's notes.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to January 14, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
